content: The linguistic double meaning of "reflexion" (both the physical act of light reflection and deliberate cognitive self-evaluation in high school English curricula) can be used to design more grounded LLM self-reflection mechanisms that separate the passive "reflection" of existing model outputs from active critical self-evaluation. This dual framing reveals that current LLM self-reflection frameworks often only implement the light-reflection sense of the term, mapping existing model biases back to outputs instead of doing the deliberate, evaluative sense of reflection that defines the Reflexion agent framework.
